full inversion tillage with low residue retention reduces surface SOC and accelerates decomposition; apply conservative bounds (multiplier_low ≈ 0.75, multiplier_high ≈ 0.95). Ramp effect over 5 years to reflect gradual SOC decline under continued practice. Evidence: national/regional CSA reviews and tillage meta‑analyses.
typical conventional tillage; moderate SOC loss risk relative to reduced‑till. Use lower bound for crediting; multiplier bounds ≈ 0.82–1.05. Ramp 5 years.

reduced till combined with cover cropping can increase net ecosystem C balance, but effectiveness is site‑specific (soil texture, cover crop species, biomass). Use monitoring triggers (documented cover crop biomass, residue retention) before applying full multipliers; multiplier bounds ≈ 0.95–1.30. Ramp 7 years to reflect slower SOC accrual and interannual variability. Recent California studies show cover crop + reduced till benefits depend strongly on texture and local conditions.
